title: Bridge Essay: The neural bases of argumentative reasoning.
concept: Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.

**The neural bases of argumentative reasoning.** - Brain Lang (2020). DOI: 10.1016/j.bandl.2020.104827. PMID: 32590183. Most reasoning tasks used in behavioral and neuroimaging studies are abstract, triggering slow, effortful processes. By contrast, most of everyday life reasoning is fast and effortless, as when we exchange arguments in conversation. Recent behavioral studies have shown that reasoning tasks with the same underlying logic can be solved much more easily if they are embedded in an argumentative context. In the present article, we study the neural bases of this type of everyday, argumentative reasoni
